title: GUIDELINES ON THE USE OF TECHNOLOGY TO PROVIDE FOOD INFORMATION

sections.section: GUIDELINES ON THE USE OF TECHNOLOGY TO PROVIDE FOOD INFORMATION
sections.summary: Document title: 'Guidelines on the Use of Technology to Provide Food Information'.
Document identifier: 'IN FOOD LABELLING CXG 105-2024' (Codex guideline code and year).
Purpose: provide guidance on the use of technology to deliver food information to consumers about pre‑packaged foods.
Scope: these guidelines apply to food information accessed by consumers via technology through a reference on a pre‑packaged food's label or labelling.
Use: the guidelines should be read in conjunction with Codex texts related to pre‑packaged food labelling, including the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

Defines terms for these guidelines: 'Food information' is the information that is the subject of a Codex text about a pre-packaged food, and 'Technology' means any electronic or digital means (e.g., websites, online platforms, mobile applications).

sections.section: 5. CONSIDERATIONS FOR DECIDING IF INFORMATION REQUIRED ON A PRE-PACKAGED FOOD LABEL OR LABELLING COULD INSTEAD BE PROVIDED TO CONSUMERS USING TECHNOLOGY
sections.summary: Sets criteria for when label-required information may instead be provided via technology: it must be readily accessible during normal purchase/use (adequate infrastructure, widespread access/adoption, and reasonable consumer use), and prohibits exclusive use of technology for the food name, safety/nutrition or other mandatory information determined by the competent authority and for individual-product information (e.g., lot code, date marking) if exclusive use would prevent relating the information to that product.

sections.section: 6. USE OF TECHNOLOGY TO PROVIDE CONSUMERS ACCESS TO FOOD INFORMATION THAT IS NOT ACCESSIBLE ON THE LABEL
sections.summary: States that when labelling information is not accessible to consumers due to sale conditions or labelling exemptions, consideration should be given to using technology to provide access to that information.

sections.section: 7. PRINCIPLES THAT ARE APPLICABLE WHEN FOOD INFORMATION IS PROVIDED TO CONSUMERS USING TECHNOLOGY
sections.summary: Sets principles for providing food information via technology: apply general Codex labelling principles; information must not conflict with label content and, if mandatory, the label reference must link directly and present mandatory information together and distinctly; online information must comply with applicable Codex texts, be available for the product's safe/useful period (including up to a use-by date), be accessible without consumers disclosing personal data, enable clear identification that the information pertains to that pre-packaged food, and be provided free of charge, in suitable language and in a clear, prominent, legible (or audible) format, with an explanatory statement on the label if the reference is not self-explanatory.
